message:
🌱 clickhouse: Local cache for remote filesystem (RFC) (#1108)

* 🌱 Add clickhouse: Local cache for remote filesystem (RFC) mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🐛 Fix vault-1468 mission: correct resolution to reflect PATCH KV v2 support

The resolution incorrectly quoted a rejection comment ("it's not going to
happen") but the feature WAS implemented via PR #12687 (merged Oct 2021).
Updated resolution summary, step 3, and added PR link to sourceUrls.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* Fix accuracy: correct PR link and difficulty for clickhouse#28961

- Changed PR URL from closed #29638 to merged #33717 which actually
  implemented the local cache for remote filesystem feature
- Changed difficulty from "beginner" to "advanced" (LRU cache with
  eviction policies is not a beginner-level feature)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

+        "description": "Review the relevant clickhouse configuration:\nA directory on local filesystem can be set up to hold a cache for remote filesystem up to specified size."

+        "title": "Apply the fix for Local cache for remote filesystem (RFC)",
+        "description": "Changelog category (leave one):\n- New Feature\n\nChangelog entry (a user-readable short description of the changes that goes to CHANGELOG.md):\n- Local disk cache for data stored on S3-compatible storage\n\nDetailed description / Documentation draft:\nIssue #28961 \nLocal disk cache for data stored on S3-compatible storage\nSettings in storage_configuration.disks.<disk_name>\n- data_cache_enabled - true/false - turn on/off local cache for this disk\n- data_cache_path - string - path for cache directory,\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },

@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "vault-1468-patch-api-needed-for-updating-multiple-secrets-stored-at-a-single-uri",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "vault: PATCH API needed for updating multiple secrets stored at a single URI path",
+    "description": "PATCH API needed for updating multiple secrets stored at a single URI path. Requested by 34+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current vault setup",
+        "description": "Verify your vault version and configuration:\n```bash\nvault version\n```\nThis feature requires a working vault installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review vault configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant vault configuration:\nCurrently, Vault does not have any PATCH API for updating single fields when storing multiple fields at a given URI path.  A common use case is to just update a single value."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Enable KV v2 and use the PATCH operation",
+        "description": "Vault added HTTP PATCH support for the KV v2 secrets engine (PR #12687, merged October 2021). To use it, ensure KV v2 is enabled and use the PATCH method to update individual fields:\n```bash\n# Enable KV v2 if not already enabled\nvault secrets enable -version=2 kv\n# Use vault kv patch to update a single field without overwriting others\nvault kv patch kv/myapp dbhost=newhost\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade vault to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade vault:\n```bash\n# Check current version\nvault version\n# Follow the project's upgrade guide at https://github.com/hashicorp/vault\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"PATCH API needed for updating multiple secrets stored at a…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Vault implemented HTTP PATCH support for the KV v2 secrets engine in PR #12687 (merged October 2021). This allows users to update individual fields at a given secret path without overwriting the entire secret. The `vault kv patch` CLI command and the HTTP PATCH method on the KV v2 API enable partial updates.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "{{ with secret \"secret/php\"}}\n              $dbhost = '{{ .Data.dbhost }}';\n              $dbuser = '{{ .Data.dbuser }}';\n              $dbpass = '{{ .Data.dbpass }}';\n              $dbname = '{{ .Data.dbname }}';\n{{ end }}",
+        "{{ with secret \"secret/php/dbhost\"}}\n              $dbhost = '{{ .Data.value }}';{{ end }}\n{{ with secret \"secret/php/dbuser\"}}\n              $dbuser = '{{ .Data.value }}';{{ end }}\n{{ with secret \"secret/php/dbpass\"}}\n              $dbpass = '{{ .Data.value }}';{{ end }}\n{{ with secret \"secret/php/dbname\"}}\n              $dbname = '{{ .Data.value }}';{{ end }}",
+        "# vault write /secret/php/dbhost value=xxx \n# vault write /secret/php/dbpass value=yyy \n# vault write /secret/php/dbuser value=www\n# vault write /secret/php/dbname value=zzz"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "vault",
+      "community",
+      "secrets",
+      "feature"
+    ],

+    ],
